127.0.0.11filterednanhighTruepolicy violation — non-443 exposureTCP/1 (well-known system port) is reachable. Enterprise policy permits HTTPS/443 only; any other open or filtered port is a potential lateral-movement entry point and must be closed, justified, or moved behind a bastion.Adversaries fingerprint the listening service and pivot via known CVEs, default credentials, or protocol-level weaknesses.Close the port at the host firewall and the perimeter, or document the exception with compensating controls (mTLS, allow-list, MFA, segmentation).['CWE-1188', 'CIS Controls 4.5', 'NIST 800-53 SC-7']NoneNoneFalsesocket-probe

127.0.0.121filteredftphighTruecleartext credentials & data exfiltrationFile Transfer Protocol transmits usernames, passwords, and payload data in cleartext.Passive sniffing of the network captures credentials; anonymous-FTP misconfiguration leaks files; banner-grabbing fingerprints daemon for known CVEs.Disable or replace with SFTP (port 22 inside a bastion) or FTPS over TLS 1.2+; block 21/tcp at the perimeter.['CWE-319', 'CVE-2010-4221', 'CIS Controls 4.5', 'MITRE ATT&CK T1021']NoneNoneFalsesocket-probe
127.0.0.122filteredsshhighTrueremote interactive shellSecure Shell allows interactive remote administration. Exposure to the internet enables credential brute-force and lateral movement after compromise.Username enumeration, credential spraying, weak key reuse, agent-forwarding abuse, supply-chain key theft.Restrict to bastion hosts behind a VPN; enforce key-based auth + hardware MFA; deny root login; fail2ban; audit authorized_keys.['CWE-307', 'CIS Benchmark §5.2', 'MITRE ATT&CK T1021.004', 'NIST 800-53 AC-17']NoneNoneFalsesocket-probe
127.0.0.123filteredtelnethighTruecleartext remote shellTelnet provides interactive shell access with no encryption; all session traffic including passwords is sniffable.Network sniffing, MITM, banner-grabbing for embedded-device exploitation.Decommission entirely; replace with SSH; block 23/tcp at every boundary.['CWE-319', 'CWE-326', 'CIS Controls 4.5']NoneNoneFalsesocket-probe

127.0.0.153filtereddnshighTrueDNS amplification & zone transferDNS over an open resolver enables reflection/amplification DDoS and zone-walking for reconnaissance.Recursive abuse for DDoS amplification; AXFR zone transfer leaking the internal namespace; DNS rebinding.Disable recursion for external clients; restrict AXFR to trusted secondaries; enable DNSSEC; firewall 53/tcp inbound.['CWE-406', 'US-CERT TA13-088A']NoneNoneFalsesocket-probe

127.0.0.1161filteredsnmphighTruedevice management information disclosureSNMPv1/v2c uses community strings (often "public"/"private") in cleartext.Community-string guessing reveals routes, ARP tables, configs; SNMP write community enables config tampering.Use SNMPv3 with auth+priv; rotate community strings; restrict to management VLAN; firewall 161/162 externally.['CWE-521', 'CIS Controls 12.6']NoneNoneFalsesocket-probe

127.0.0.1445opensmbcriticalTrueremote file sharing & RCE chainSMB over TCP exposes file shares and is a recurring RCE target.EternalBlue (MS17-010), SMBGhost (CVE-2020-0796), relay attacks against NTLM.Block 445/tcp at the perimeter; disable SMBv1; enforce SMB signing and channel binding.['CVE-2017-0144', 'CVE-2020-0796', 'MITRE ATT&CK T1021.002']NoneNoneTruesocket-probe

127.0.0.1512filteredrexechighTruelegacy Berkeley remote shellrexec authenticates with cleartext credentials and is universally deprecated.Credential capture; legacy buffer-overflow CVEs.Remove rexec/rlogin/rsh; replace with SSH; block 512-514/tcp.['CVE-1999-0651']NoneNoneFalsesocket-probe
127.0.0.1513filteredrloginhighTruelegacy trust-based remote login.rhosts trust files allow passwordless logins from any host that can spoof an address.IP spoofing into the trust list; cleartext session sniffing.Decommission; replace with SSH key auth.['CVE-1999-0651']NoneNoneFalsesocket-probe
127.0.0.1514filteredsyslog/rshhighTruecleartext logging & legacy shellUDP/TCP 514 carries unauthenticated syslog or the rsh remote shell.Log spoofing/forgery; cleartext credentials for rsh.Use TLS-protected syslog (RFC 5425) or HTTPS-based log shipping; remove rsh entirely.['RFC 5425', 'CWE-319']NoneNoneFalsesocket-probe

127.0.0.11521openoracle-tnscriticalTrueOracle TNS ListenerOracle Transparent Network Substrate exposes service names and version banners and has a long CVE history.TNS poisoning (CVE-2012-1675), credential brute-force, listener command injection.Apply Oracle CPU; enable VALID_NODE_CHECKING; restrict listener to private network.['CVE-2012-1675']NoneNoneTruesocket-probe

127.0.0.12375filtereddockerhighTrueunauthenticated Docker APIDocker daemon socket on 2375 grants root-equivalent container control with no authentication.Container escape, host filesystem mount, cryptominer drop.Never expose 2375; use 2376 with mTLS only on management networks; prefer Unix socket + sudo.['CWE-306', 'MITRE ATT&CK T1610']NoneNoneFalsesocket-probe
127.0.0.12376filtereddocker-tlshighTrueDocker API (TLS)Even with TLS, the Docker API equals root on the host and should never be internet-facing.Client certificate theft yields full container/host control.Restrict to management VLAN; rotate client certs; prefer SSH+kubectl/docker context.['CWE-306']NoneNoneFalsesocket-probe

127.0.0.13306filteredmysqlhighTruedatabase engine exposedMySQL/MariaDB bound to a public interface enables credential attacks and data exfiltration.Brute-force of root/app users, UDF privilege escalation, replication abuse.Bind to 127.0.0.1; require TLS; rotate credentials; firewall 3306; use bastion + tunnel.['CWE-284', 'CIS MySQL Benchmark']NoneNoneFalsesocket-probe
